name: Lockerley
overview: Lockerley is a village in the Test Valley in Hampshire, on the southern bank of the River Dun about 6 miles north-west of Romsey and 11 miles from Salisbury. It is an attractive collection of greens (centred on Butts Green) with a strong community spirit and around 35 clubs and societies. The 12th-century church of St John the Evangelist is a fine example of Norman architecture, and the village was historically part of the landholdings of Romsey Abbey.
character: A scenic, green-centred Test Valley village with a notably active community and a semi-rural feel, surrounded by rolling hills and ancient woodland.
amenities: Village store with pop-up Post Office and coffee shop
St John the Evangelist Church (12th-century Norman) and a second church
Active community/village hall and around 35 groups and societies
The Mill Arms pub in neighbouring Dunbridge
Holbury Lakes Trout Fishery on the village edge

schools.name: Romsey/Test Valley secondary schools (e.g. Romsey School, Mountbatten)

commutes.notes: Via Mottisfont & Dunbridge / Dean stations and Salisbury or Romsey

prices.summary: A desirable Test Valley village of period cottages and detached family homes around the greens, with values supported by the school, community and River Dun setting. See Rightmove for current sold-price detail.

thingsToDo: Trout fishing at Holbury Lakes and on the River Dun
Countryside walks and rides in the Test Valley
St John the Evangelist Church (Norman, with a 14th-century wall painting)
Walks to neighbouring Dunbridge and the River Test
pros: Excellent village school and strong community (around 35 groups)
Village store, coffee shop and garage retained
Beautiful Test Valley setting with fishing and walks
Nearby rail at Mottisfont & Dunbridge / Dean
cons: No pub in the village itself (nearest at Dunbridge)
Broadband can be slow in places (median ~18 Mbps in parts)
Car-dependent for secondary schooling and larger shops
whoItSuits: Families wanting a friendly, green-centred Test Valley village with a strong school and countryside on the doorstep.
